title: Hub and Eurocities release Local Good Practices Catalogue
content:
Following the announcement of the 10 initiatives selected from the Open Call for Local Good Practices in March 2026, the EU-funded European Heritage Hub has officially released the complete Local Good Practices Factsheets Catalogue. This newly available resource turns the initial selection into an actionable toolkit. Developed in collaboration with Eurocities, the catalogue includes 10 detailed case-study factsheets that serve as transferable approaches and policy solutions for local authorities, heritage professionals, and community advocates looking to lead the green, digital, and social transformation across European cities and regions.

While the selection highlights the geographic and thematic diversity of practices from across Europe, this publication allows the wider heritage community to dive deep into the specific methodologies, governance structures, and measurable impacts of the initiatives.
snippet: Following the announcement of the 10 initiatives selected from the Open Call for Local Good Practices in March 2026, the EU-funded European Heritage Hub has officially released the complete Local Good
creator: Liv Martin

title: Hub’s New European Bauhaus Festival Satellite Event highlights cultural heritage-led solutions to Europe’s housing and climate challenges
content:
The European Heritage Hub’s official satellite event of the New European Bauhaus Festival, “Cultural Heritage as a Resource for Energy-Efficient and Inclusive Renovation and Affordable Housing Policies”, brought together policymakers, heritage experts, architects, local authorities and finance professionals to explore how cultural heritage can contribute to energy-efficient renovation, affordable housing and climate resilience.

The event included the participation of Hub consortium partners Eurocities and ICLEI, and members of the European Heritage Alliance, including the Architects’ Council of Europe (ACE) and the International Council on Monuments and Sites (ICOMOS). It built on the Hub’s December 2025 Heritage Dialogues webinar,  “Cultural Heritage and Building Renovation: Aligning the EPBD and Affordable Housing Objectives”.
snippet: The European Heritage Hub’s official satellite event of the New European Bauhaus Festival, “Cultural Heritage as a Resource for Energy-Efficient and Inclusive Renovation and Affordable Housing
creator: Liv Martin
